TextView.android:text: Simple anonymous FTP access to shared storage. Use passive mode in the FTP client.
Sleep keeps CPU/Wi-Fi alive; background keeps FTP off-screen.
Simple passive FTP client with local files on the left and server files on the right.
Simple HTTP file server for a local web root. HTTPS works with a PKCS12 certificate file.
Sleep keeps CPU/Wi-Fi alive; background keeps HTTP and the Web Interface off-screen.
Basic browser remote control hosted by the HTTP server. Only enabled Web Interface sections and supported actions are exposed.
Global covers Server Controls, Status, Output, and other app-level actions. Tab checkboxes gate tab-specific web sections as they are added.
Foreground SSH/SFTP access over the local network. A persistent notification stays active while the server is running.
SFTP uses the configured root folder. Shell access starts a normal app-owned shell, not root.
Connect to an SSH host and run a command from the Network tab. Password auth is supported in this first pass.
LAN link controls for sharing selected app surfaces and letting a guest device request approved host actions. Enable it first in Settings > Main.
Choose what a connected guest can request. Advanced capability flags are advertised only and do not run host actions by themselves.
Connect first. Buttons enable only when the host advertises that action.
Run common Android network inspection commands through the selected backend.
Ping, DNS lookup, or quick route lookup for a hostname/IP.
Quick HEAD/GET checks using whichever network tool is available on the device.
Attempts a short TCP connect test using nc/toybox nc when available.
